coreConcept: The water cycle is a natural process that describes how water moves around our planet. It includes the stages of evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection, helping to keep our environment healthy and water available.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun warms the Earth's surface, causing water to evaporate into the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds form when water vapor cools down and condenses into tiny droplets.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Rain
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Rain falls back to the ground when the clouds get too heavy, providing water to plants and animals.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Rivers
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Rivers carry water from the land back to the oceans, completing the cycle.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Groundwater
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Groundwater is stored in the soil and rocks underground, providing a source of water for plants and people.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation
storyOfProcess.description: Water from oceans, rivers, and lakes heats up from the Sun and turns into vapor, rising into the sky.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: As the water vapor cools, it turns back into tiny water droplets, forming clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When the clouds get heavy with water droplets, they release the water as rain or other forms of precipitation.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection
storyOfProcess.description: The rainwater collects in rivers, lakes, and the ground, ready to be evaporated again.

corePrinciples.name: Energy Transfer
corePrinciples.description: Energy from the Sun drives the water cycle, highlighting how energy is crucial for natural processes.

corePrinciples.name: Conservation of Water
corePrinciples.description: The water cycle shows us how water is constantly recycled, emphasizing the importance of saving this precious resource.

corePrinciples.name: Weather Patterns
corePrinciples.description: Understanding the water cycle helps us predict weather and climate changes, which is vital for our daily lives.
